summary: Jeff Atwood reflects on his heavy daily use of the basic Windows Notepad and his discovery of Notepad2 as a superior replacement. Despite Notepad's severely limited functionality, it had remained a core part of his developer toolkit for years. Notepad2 maintains the original's philosophy of being small and fast while adding substantial features like syntax highlighting for numerous languages, regular expressions, and Unicode support. The post celebrates finding a tool that enhances functionality without sacrificing the simplicity that made the original valuable.
key_points: Notepad.exe, despite its limitations, is used dozens of times daily as a core developer tool
Notepad2 adds substantial features while only increasing size from ~100kb to 550kb
The best tools follow the principle of being small, fast, and without unnecessary frills
Key additions include syntax highlighting for 20+ languages, regex search, and Unicode support
money_quotes: Given the severely limited functionality of Notepad, it's incredible that it has taken me this long to find a suitable replacement for what is, evidently, a core part of my developer toolkit.
The original Notepad shipped with Windows is probably the handiest program of all times, small, fast, without frills!
Look at everything you get for that additional 450kb.
key_insight: The best developer tools maintain simplicity and speed while thoughtfully adding functionality—proving that lightweight doesn't have to mean featureless.
